Well, to learn a little bit more about this gentleman, let's check out this short video. Today we're in the company of a phenomenal success story. It is everything that Kevin Costner movie when he was, remember the Kevin Costner movie, feel the dreams? The whole concept of the movie was if you build it, they will come. That's how people feel so much with their businesses. If I make it strong enough, if I write the good enough book, if I can impact the world, if my coaching is the best, if my product is the best, they will come. Guess what? They will never come. It's a delusion people have every once in a blue moon, there's something that goes viral on its own. That's one out of a million. Every other company you see has got a marketing machine kicking ass behind the scenes. Think about it. Was Mother Teresa a saleswoman? Was Martin Luther King a salesman? Hell yes. There was there was amazing nuns that wanted to help people. There was the civil rights movement had lots of great leaders, but why they stick out? Because they were too bad ass sales people. So selling is the fuel. It's the oxygen for your next level. All right. So now I want to share what I believe to be the secret to my success. And again, I'm not kidding. I'm not just saying this. I've sold millions of books on TV and online. I converted. I took all my strategies from TV. I ignored online like an idiot until a year ago. Most of you probably never even saw me online until a year ago, but now we're dominating, right? So I took these same strategies and they work in direct mail. They work on TV. They work on YouTube. They work on Facebook. They work on Instagram. So that's what I'm sharing. And one thing before I get to this part that I think is the framework for what's worked so well. The person you have to do a sales job on, and maybe this is a little personal growth mixed in, and I think that's the secret in my life, is the person you have to do the biggest sales job on is you. When you love what you offer to other people so much that you feel like you're doing them a disservice if you don't get their credit card. When you understand that and understand your mission, your passion, your desire, the person you have to sell the most every day is you. And if that's looking in the mirror and getting yourself in that moment of that part, that enthusiastic, that authentic version of you, then do it. If you have to read your goals every day, do it. If you have to write down new goals once a week, do it. Because I don't know if you realize this. How we are is how the people around us are. If you're in this room, you're a leader. You're a leader in your community, you're a leader in your business, you're a leader online. How you react, how you come across this, how you set the tone around you. And if you're having a bad day because you didn't sell yourself that day, and you doubt your own success, you doubt your ability to grow, you doubt your next level, even 5%, guess what? People smell it. You could smell lack of confidence. I can pick it out in someone in two minutes. People pitch me all the time. I could tell when someone's pitching me, and they're desperate, and they need me for money. And I could tell when someone's pitching me because a 1 plus 1 equals 10. I know the difference in three seconds and so do you. So if you're not selling yourself every single day, then you're doing a disservice to the people around you, the people, your partners, your friends, your family. So know your mission. Know where you want to go, and sell yourself on a non-stop basis. And the last thing I'll say before I get to this little section I want to do for you is we are at a phase in the world, I believe, and I have a little history of this, 20 years every day on TV and the internet, over a billion dollars in sales. I'm reminding you of that because I want you to listen to this. It's we're at a phase where people are sick of the bullshit for lack of a better word. People are sick of the magic money machine where you get rich in five minutes. People are sick with the pill that'll get you, have a ripped stomach without doing the work. We are done with it. That phase came, I watched it come on TV 15 years ago. My team freaked out, look what everybody's saying. I said they'll be gone. If you guys watch my ads, do I always say it doesn't spit out money? It's not a magic money machine. You got to do the work. If you're going to buy this book and think to get rich, don't buy my book. The more authentic you are, the more real you are, the more raw you are, is what the world is craving. Everybody's sick of the shit. So you couldn't be sitting in those chairs in my belief at any better time in history because right now the world, you know what they want? They just want you. Don't try to be Russell on camera. Don't try to be me on camera. Don't try to be anybody. Be the best version of you that you could be right now and believe me. I look back at that video. So now I want to take a little different direction, but go deeper on what Russell does so well. And that's the three steps. Hook, story, and close. I think Russell says, offer there. I've been teaching this for a long time, thinking through this for a long time. And again, everything Russell talks about on this was right and spot on. But I want to give you a little example so it really anchors in because here's the thing. Everybody, most people are really good at this. I watch videos obsessively, just like Russell, just like so many of you. And I'll watch so many people be great with the story, talking about their passions, their desires. They're automatically overcoming obstacles for the end buyer in advance. Their response blocking in advance. They're building authenticity. I mean, they're building credibility. They're getting you to trust them. All the things that need to go in a story, you want to overcome their obstacles, you want to build trust, you want to show why you're the authority. All those things happen here. I'm not even going to go deep in here. You tell your story, you get, you anchor them in. So most people are good with the story. And a story will get you a nice little six-figure business. But it's not going to take you where you want to go without the hook and the close. I'm going to tell you a quick little story. So I, about God, this is a long time ago, I was watching Larry King probably 10 or 12 years ago, and he's interviewing Joel Osteen. And it gets to a certain point. And Larry looks at Joel and it was like one of those moments on TV. And he said, Joel, I'm a Jew. I don't believe in Jesus. I don't believe Jesus is the Son of God. Am I going to hell? And I was like, oh, that's like, oh my God, I was hiding. And I wasn't in the audience. I was home and I was hiding. And Joel is just a sweet guy. I've had the chance to hang out with him a few times. He's like, I'm not going to play God. I can't give you that answer. But here's the epiphany I had as an obsessive direct response marketer. All I thought about is, oh my God, what if there was a call to action right there? What if, what if Larry King? What if Larry King, in Joel's embarrassing moment, I'm thinking, what if Larry King looked into the camera and said, like Joel or not, you have the chance to get his book right here on the show. Call the number. That's all I could think about. I swear to you, I didn't sleep for days. I geeked out. I bought the Larry King set. I bought the desk like Larry King. I bought the Larry King microphone for five grand. And I put it all on. I set it all up and I hire a guy. Now my company was doing good. This is 2007. So this is 12 years ago. 2007. I hire a guy to interview me and he says, give me the questions. What's the teleprompter? I said, you know what, let's pretend this is a real interview. And what I want you to do is act like the market's crashing. I don't know anything about real estate. I'm just some late night huckster. And he's like, you want me to do that? Anyway, we live this experience and that infomercial changed my life. I did the first direct to consumer infomercial for a book. It was for my book, Be A Real Estate Millionaire. That infomercial changed my life. That infomercial did $140 million in sales. And I sold a million copies of that book. And it all came from Larry and Joel. So I fast forward. I say that two years ago, Larry King, we meet through a mutual friend. He's at my office. I asked him if he could do an infomercial with me. He says, yes, I'm ecstatic. We pull out the old set. We pull out the old microphone and Larry King comes to my office and I'm like, like a little kid, I was nervous to go on camera for the first time. He walks in and he's like, he's an awesome guy, but he's like, he's just setting his ways. He walks over and he looks at the set and he's like, why would the microphone be there? It belongs here. And he moved at like seven inches. And I felt like I was scolded because I had the microphone in the wrong spot. So anyway, long story short, Larry and I do an infomercial. And what I did is I obsessed. I totally obsessed on all his best interviews. I geeked out on his interview with Prince and Madonna and Gorbachev and Presidents. And I watched his open and Larry always started with a tonight on Larry King. I'm here with, if you guys are a little older and remember, it was Larry King interviewed all the best. So I'm sitting across from him and I said, no, just let's have this great interview. And he says, tonight I write his open. I write the hook. And I had obsessed on hooks on TV. You guys in today's world, you get to market to your demographic. Infomercials, I'm casting a big wide net. I'm just hoping somebody doesn't skip the commercial. They walk by the TV and then they stop and listen to me. I don't know if it's a mom, a child, an adult. Don't know anything, right? So I had to be really obsessive on a hook to stop the channel. Anyway, long story short, Larry King does this open. I'm going to show you. Want to see it? Okay. Let's play that. This is Larry King. Tonight, my guest is Dean Brasio. He's a multiple New York Times bestselling author, success trainer, speaking to so many crowds around the world. Who in here wants more love? More passion? Who wants more money? Okay. Okay. You take that down. Okay. So now I do this show and I'm in heaven. Larry King, this guy that changed my life. He didn't know it until I told him the story. He changes my life. I tell him the story. He's excited. He goes to interview me and I write that open. Just like I was Prince or Madonna or Reagan back in the day. And I totally screwed up. No one really gives a shit about me. I went against what I know is people buy from you. They learn from you. They adore you when they feel understood, not when they understand you. If you've never heard that, write that down. Right now it will change your life. People learn from you, buy from you, trust you when they feel understood, not when they understand you. And what did I do? I went against everything I know and I made it about me. I'm a New York Times best-selling author. I've done this. I've done that. I'm amazing. I'm cool. No one cares. What's in it for them? So I'm obsessing on this information. Why is it not working? It's my fate. It's Larry King. We're having this great conversation. The call to action is good. The interview is good. And I watch it and I tweak it and nothing. And then I watch it and it hit me. It hit me like a ton of bricks. I messed up on the hook. I messed up on this. Listen, on infomercials, I used to think I had a minute or two to hook somebody. You don't have a minute or two anymore. You got a moment to see if people will take action. A moment in a sea of red shirts. How do you be the only black one that stands out? How does someone stop at your ad? Look at your ad. We have to hook them to stop. So we have to ask a relevant question. So I obsessed on one thing. Larry King's opening sentence. It's nothing else changed with the show. And I thought, what is relevant to a lot of people? And I came up with this. And it works. I would fit everybody in this room. The opening sentence was, in your adult life, have you ever looked in the mirror and thought to yourself, wow, I thought I'd be further ahead by now. That ever happened to anybody? Anybody in that room feel that? Let me show you the difference. Let's pull that screen up. You can take it off. You see the difference to those two? Thank you. One is about you. One's about your emotions, your fears, your thoughts, what's going on. Enter, write this down too. We need to enter conversations already going on in the mind of our prospect. We get so excited about what we do. I got so excited to do an interview with Larry King. All I thought about was me. All I thought about was my success, my books. It's not about me. It's not about you. It's about them. And I was living in my head. When I changed it and lived in their head, everything changed. I changed. I flew Larry back in. I begged him to please do the show over just the opening. We put the same shirt on, put the same clothes on, put them in the same set. We changed the first three sentences of the show. That show is running right now. It's over $40 million in sales. The hook. So I want you guys to, I hope I'm sharing something a little different or a little different look of what Russell did. But I want you guys to obsess on what comes out of your mouth immediately. Listen, there's hooks and grabbers. I've also done some cool stuff. If anybody saw me pulling a book out of my Tesla dashboard, I had a box where books just fell out. But mostly it's never that. It's always, I'm always a word, I like to be a word smith. I'm always thinking, what is the biggest pain point of my prospect that they're facing right this second and how can I get them to stop and go, yeah, I've looked in the mirror and thought to myself, wow. My first infomercial, not my first, the one I did after Larry King, the first sit down infomercial, that's when the economy crashed. And it was when we, I think Obama put like $700 billion into the economy. To what everybody was talking about, my opening line was, hey, I know Obama put $700 billion into the economy. How much of that landed in your bank account? That was the opening line. That show was a monster. So I just want to talk, I'm not going to talk much about the story today. I want to obsess on the hook. Go back and look at some of your videos that you love and they didn't work. And I bet you you'll find what I did. Unfortunately, about five IT people lost their job because of them. But oh well. All right. Before I go to this next part, I really want you to think about the stamina. And I want you to think of the terms and you should write this down. Do you have marketing stamina? Planes fly. When a plane crashes, you don't question the aerodynamics. You don't say, oh, planes, planes just, it's metal. How could it get off the ground? You know that do, can we all agree that planes fly? So if it crashes, you don't question the aerodynamics of the plane, do you? But when an ad doesn't work, you question if marketing works. Facebook is oversaturated. That doesn't work. No, it works for me every single day. It works for half of you in this audience. Just keep telling yourself, I need the stamina to continue because I have a great product. I love what I do and I'm making this work. When you do that, then do whatever it takes. If you hit an obstacle, go through it. If you hit 40 more obstacles, go to 41 because you'll crack the code and your life will change forever. Marketing is the oxygen of your next level. All right. So now I want to go to, let me go to this story. I told you, this is where I would bet to say most of you are already good with the story. So I'm going to jump forward and go right to the close. I know Russell goes deep on the structure of the clothes. I want to talk about this little space that lives right here. Now, if you're doing an ad, it's shorter. But if you're doing a sales video, if you're doing a webinar, if you're doing an automated sales presentation, like if you go to my get my book, that's when you go from a hook, you come out with a big hook and somebody says, oh, I like that. I'm staying. And then you prove your value, you prove your worth. And this is the second part most people screw up is going to the close. This is the part. Did you ever watch anybody on stage who's up here on fire? They're shared. They hooked you in, you know their credibility. They're telling their story and like, yeah, I want to do what he does. I want to do what she does. And you're all excited. And then they're going to present something to you. And they're like this. And then they go to sell and they're like, and you know, I got a couple slides. And if you want to get my stuff, you can. They're physiology changes. And they apologize for the sale. And they wonder why it didn't work. It's like, I'm sorry. I'm going to offer you something. You just wowed me and now I'm not buying from you. You have no confidence in your own thing. Listen, a great example. One of my companies that I own with Brennan Bashard is called growth. We invited Tony Horton to come speak P90X. He lit the audience up and he asked us if he could sell. I said, sure, go ahead. We weren't really letting anybody else, but it was Tony. He's impacted so many lives. So he's up there and he's on fire. The audience is loving him. And literally, I just had that presentation a day before about fall in love with selling. He literally was like, and he's like, and I built this course. You know, I put my whole life into it. And if you want, you know, I hate to sell. I hate to sell. That's why, you know, and we had 2,500 people in the room. He apologized for the sale, told people the good news. Told people to go to the back at a 2,500 people. How many people you think bought a $450 course that he obsessed over? Two. Two. He had the whole audience. He went from the story. People start freaking out. I don't know about you guys. You ever have who in here spoke on stage before? Okay, so many of you. You ever realized you have two voices going on? Like you're talking and then you're like, why is the guy in the third row over half asleep? Am I screwing this up? Like, bro, why are you talking to your buddy? Look at your text one more time. I'm coming out and getting you. Like, how do you do that? And I'm still talking and that conversation is going on. Well, that same conversation goes on for people when they're getting ready to sell. Talk about your life, your passion, your heart. And it's like, oh no, I have to go to my slides. Shit. And it's like, and so, you know, and you could like, you have to think about that. That's when everything goes to hell in a hand basket. What you have to do is take and carry that enthusiasm. Don't apologize. Listen, when Russell comes out and he's already offered some great stuff, whatever Russell offers, I hope he's standing on the edge here, getting you to be engaged because it's the only way he could change your life. And you're crazy if you don't get involved. And I'm being honest. I buy everything the guy comes up with. He buys everything I come up with too. So it's pretty cool. Mine are more expensive. What? Anyway. So I'm going to give you guys, I'm going to give you guys a little, I'm going to give you guys a little, I'm going to give you a little hack. Thanks. I'm going to give you guys a little hack right now. You ready for this? Between the story and the clothes. Between the story and the clothes, build a bridge. And the bridge will be your crutch. Right here, have a bridge. And what do I mean by a bridge? I just didn't listen. There's probably people who have been doing this for years. I'm just sharing. So I never heard it for me. When I get done with the story part and it's time to sell, I want my enthusiasm to go up. I want to step into it. I want you to know that I believe in what I do. I put my heart and soul into this. So I find a way to come up with a bridge that I know that's the trigger and the inside voice that goes, hell yes, it's time to freaking sell. Hell yes, it's time to impact lives. And when that voice is talking, I get people to run to the back of the room. I get people to buy on my webinar, on my livecast, on my videos. And the bridge is something like this. So you say you're sharing up to a certain point. One that I've used, I'm going to share one. You'll see it if you ever on one of my webinars or webcasts. I'll say, hey, listen, you've been with me for an hour. You've probably seen my books. Maybe you've read my book, watch me on Instagram. I'm betting by now you know my values. You know what I stand for. You know how I communicate. You know how I teach. And here's what I know. If you want another level of life, you're going to need help. There's no way you're going to get to the level you want on your own. I'd like to be that help. And that's why I'm so excited to offer you this.
